title: Verify working directory before destructive shell commands
description: Ran a recursive delete in what I thought was a scratch directory; it was the project root. Lost an hour of uncommitted work.
context: Shell sessions span multiple projects; `cd` history is invisible after a context switch.
resolution: Restored from git; the unstaged files were gone.
prevention: Before any `rm -rf`, `git reset --hard`, `git clean -fd`, `find -delete`, or similar: print `pwd` and `ls` and confirm the target. If the action is irreversible and affects more than one file, ask before running.
trigger: About to run a recursive or force-delete shell command
